Local Optimization: Optimize an individual process, facility, vendor, queue, or resource allocation problem
Transaction Optimization: Optimize digital transactions, platform matching, ordering, payment, and convenience
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Optimize multi-stakeholder mission achievement and ecosystem value
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: Existing approaches rarely integrate stakeholder missions, systemic value, sustainability, and resilience in one executable framework

Local Optimization: Individual manager, vendor, or operational unit
Transaction Optimization: Digital platform, marketplace operator, or recommendation algorithm
Mission-Oriented Optimization: PUDAL-controlled ecosystem governance with autonomous stakeholder agents
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE provides a coordination layer that respects stakeholder autonomy while aligning ecosystem-level missions

Transaction Optimization: Usually users and providers mediated by a platform
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Students, vendors, suppliers, delivery actors, university, operator, regulator, and external environment
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE expands analysis from dyadic transactions to full multi-stakeholder mission ecosystems

Local Optimization: Efficiency, cost reduction, throughput, profit, or utilization
Transaction Optimization: Convenience, sales, conversion, retention, rating, and platform revenue
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Systemic value combining mission achievement, satisfaction, sustainability, resilience, knowledge, and financial viability
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE formalizes systemic value through EVI and RMOV accounts

Local Optimization: Local operational data and historical records
Transaction Optimization: Platform transaction data, user behavior, order history, and payment records
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Shared ecosystem state including demand, capacity, RMOV, PSKVE, scenarios, policies, and metrics
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE integrates operational, financial, mission, environmental, and learning data into one digital twin

Local Optimization: Manual adjustment, rule-based planning, or delayed feedback
Transaction Optimization: Platform analytics, matching algorithms, and partial feedback from transactions
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Closed-loop PUDAL: Perception, Understanding, Decision, Action, Learning
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE introduces explicit ecosystem control rather than relying only on market or platform feedback

Local Optimization: Usually one decision maker or one operational function
Transaction Optimization: Usually the consumer, provider, or transaction pair
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Recommendation vector for multiple stakeholders simultaneously
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE generalizes recommender systems into MSRS for coordinated ecosystem action

Local Optimization: Mission is implicit or reduced to operational targets
Transaction Optimization: Mission is often reduced to platform growth or transaction completion
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Mission is explicit through stakeholder missions and Mission Intensity Graphs
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE makes mission intensity measurable, comparable, and synchronizable over time

Local Optimization: Material, labor, money, and output flows
Transaction Optimization: Product, service, order, payment, and rating flows
Mission-Oriented Optimization: PSKVE exchanges: Product, Service, Knowledge, Value, and Environment
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE captures non-transactional exchanges such as knowledge, public value, and environmental impact

Local Optimization: Cost, revenue, inventory, and utilization accounts
Transaction Optimization: Orders, payments, ratings, commissions, and platform metrics
Mission-Oriented Optimization: RMOV accounts: Resources, Mission, Outputs, and Value for each stakeholder
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE connects resources, mission execution, outputs, and value creation in a stakeholder-level accounting structure

Local Optimization: Often treated as a constraint or secondary metric
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Embedded in objective functions, EVI, scenarios, and evaluation metrics
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE treats sustainability as a core ecosystem performance dimension

Transaction Optimization: Platform continuity, demand response, and supply-demand matching under disturbance
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Scenario wind tunnel, stress testing, recovery analysis, and robustness ranking
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE evaluates ecosystem performance under disruptions rather than only under normal operating conditions

Local Optimization: Optimization model, spreadsheet, operations dashboard, or simulation model
Transaction Optimization: Digital platform, marketplace, recommender system, or analytics dashboard
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Digital Twin Mini-Lab with agents, PUDAL, MSRS, scenarios, RMOV, PSKVE, and EVI
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE provides a reusable engineering artifact for design, simulation, evaluation, and policy selection

Local Optimization: Analytical results, operational KPIs, and local performance comparison
Transaction Optimization: A/B testing, platform KPIs, adoption metrics, and transaction analytics
Mission-Oriented Optimization: Comparative simulation of Baseline, Online Platform, and MOEE under multiple scenarios and policies
Remaining Gap Addressed by MOEE: MOEE enables controlled comparison of ecosystem architectures before real-world deployment
